hi.heading: HCF — Bastar के बाज़ार में
hi.content: Bastar के हाट में एक किसान के पास 84 kg dhaan (धान) है और 56 kg maize है। वो equal-weight bags बनाना चाहता है — हर bag में same weight हो। Maximum कितना kg हर bag में? HCF(84, 56) = 28। हर bag में 28 kg — 3 bags dhaan के, 2 bags maize के। HCF का real use!

hi.heading: गलतफहमी: LCM हमेशा बड़ा होता है HCF से?
hi.wrong: LCM हमेशा HCF से बड़ा होता है
hi.correct: Almost always true, लेकिन exception है: जब दो numbers equal हों, जैसे 6 और 6, तो HCF = LCM = 6। General rule: LCM × HCF = Product of two numbers।

hi.heading: Indravati के Cycle से LCM
hi.content: मान लो Indravati नदी के किनारे दो boats हैं। Boat A हर 6 minutes में वापस आती है, Boat B हर 8 minutes में। दोनों एक साथ कब आएंगी? LCM(6, 8) = 24 minutes। LCM वो smallest time है जब दोनों events साथ होते हैं।

en.heading: HCF at Bastar's Haat Market
en.content: At a Bastar haat, a farmer has 84 kg of dhaan and 56 kg of maize. He wants to pack them in equal-weight bags. Maximum weight per bag? HCF(84, 56) = 28. So each bag holds 28 kg — 3 bags of dhaan, 2 of maize. That's HCF in real action.

en.heading: Is LCM Always Greater Than HCF?
en.wrong: LCM is always greater than HCF
en.correct: Almost always, but not when two numbers are equal. If both numbers are 6, then HCF = LCM = 6. The general rule is: LCM × HCF = Product of the two numbers.

en.heading: LCM with Boats on the Indravati
en.content: Imagine two boats on the Indravati river. Boat A returns every 6 minutes, Boat B every 8 minutes. When do they return together? LCM(6, 8) = 24 minutes. LCM is the smallest time when two repeating events happen simultaneously.

hi.heading: Polynomial — Bhains के दूध की Calculation
hi.content: मान लो एक bhains (भैंस) रोज़ x litres दूध देती है। एक हफ्ते में total दूध = 7x litres। अगर दूध ₹40/litre है, income = 40 × 7x = 280x। यह एक linear polynomial है। अगर दूध घटता-बढ़ता है: output = 5x² - 3x + 10 — quadratic polynomial। हर calculation polynomial है!

hi.heading: गलतफहमी: Degree मतलब Coefficients की संख्या?
hi.wrong: Polynomial की degree उसके coefficients की संख्या है
hi.correct: नहीं! Degree है highest power of variable। p(x) = 3x⁴ + 2x² + 7x + 1 की degree 4 है — चाहे terms कितनी भी हों। Power देखो, terms नहीं।

hi.heading: Zero of Polynomial — Guess करो
hi.content: p(x) = 2x - 6 लो। p(x) = 0 कब होगा? 2x - 6 = 0, x = 3। Check: p(3) = 2(3) - 6 = 0. ✓ अब try करो p(x) = x² - 9। Clue: (x-3)(x+3) = x² - 9। Zeroes क्या हैं? Graph पे x-axis crossing points!

en.heading: Polynomials — Bhains Milk Calculation
en.content: Say a bhains gives x litres of milk daily. Weekly total = 7x litres. At ₹40/litre, income = 280x. That's a linear polynomial. If output varies: 5x² - 3x + 10 — that's quadratic. Every real-life calculation is a polynomial in disguise!

en.heading: Does Degree Mean Number of Coefficients?
en.wrong: The degree of a polynomial is the count of its coefficients
en.correct: No! Degree is the highest power of the variable. In p(x) = 3x⁴ + 2x² + 7x + 1, the degree is 4. Count the power, not the terms.

en.heading: Find the Zero — Guess and Check
en.content: Take p(x) = 2x - 6. When is p(x) = 0? Solve: 2x - 6 = 0 → x = 3. Check: p(3) = 2(3) - 6 = 0. ✓ Now try p(x) = x² - 9. Hint: (x-3)(x+3) = x² - 9. What are the zeroes? They're the points where the graph crosses the x-axis!
